Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## IRC Channel Plugin - Complete Structure Analysis

I've completed a thorough exploration of the IRC channel plugin and related plugin SDK contracts. Below is the complete file structure with full contents organized by category.

---

## PART 1: ROOT-LEVEL IRC PLUGIN FILES

### /extensions/irc/package.json
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/irc",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw IRC channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": [
      "./index.ts"
    ],
    "install": {
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    },
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "irc",
      "label": "IRC",
      "selectionLabel": "IRC (Server + Nick)",
      "detailLabel": "IRC",
      "docsPath": "/channels/irc",
      "docsLabel": "irc",
      "blurb": "classic IRC networks with DM/channel routing and pairing controls.",
      "aliases": [
        "internet-relay-chat"
      ],
      "systemImage": "network"
    }
  }
}
```

### /extensions/irc/openclaw.plugin.json
```json
{
  "id": "irc",
  "channels": ["irc"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

### /extensions/irc/index.ts
```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "irc",
  name: "IRC",
  description: "IRC channel plugin",
  plugin: ircPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setIrcRuntime,
});
```

### /extensions/irc/setup-entry.ts
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(ircPlugin);
```

### /extensions/irc/api.ts
```typescript
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/setup-surface.js";
```

### /extensions/irc/channel-config-api.ts
```typescript
export { IrcChannelConfigSchema } from "./src/config-schema.js";
```

---

## PART 2: IRC RUNTIME AND TYPE DEFINITIONS

### /extensions/irc/src/types.ts
Complete type definitions for IRC configuration and messages:
- `IrcChannelConfig` - per-channel configuration (mention requirements, tools, skills, allowlist)
- `IrcNickServConfig` - NickServ identity/registration settings
- `IrcAccountConfig` - full account configuration (host, port, TLS, nick, username, password, NickServ, channels, groups, policies)
- `IrcConfig` - root config with optional multiple accounts
- `CoreConfig` - extended OpenClaw config with IRC section
- `IrcInboundMessage` - message structure with sender info, target, isGroup flag
- `IrcProbe` - connection probe result with latency

Key features:
- Support for multiple IRC accounts per config
- DM policy control (pairing, open, disabled)
- Group policy (allowlist, open, disabled)
- Per-channel configuration with mention gating
- NickServ authentication and registration
- Text chunking configuration
- Markdown table handling
- Media attachment limits

### /extensions/irc/src/runtime-api.ts
Public runtime API barrel:
Exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`:
- Status and activity recording functions
- Channel pairing controller creation
- DM and group policy resolution helpers
- Allowlist management utilities
- Message formatting and delivery
- Text chunking and inbound processing
- Plugin runtime type definitions

### /extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts
Plugin runtime store management:
```typescript
// Provides getter/setter for IRC-specific PluginRuntime instance
// Used by setup and monitor flows to access core IRC runtime services
```

---

## PART 3: CONFIGURATION AND SCHEMA

### /extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts
Zod-based configuration validation schema:
- `IrcGroupSchema` - per-channel config with validation
- `IrcNickServSchema` - NickServ config with requirement checking (email required for register=true)
- `IrcAccountSchemaBase` - base account configuration with defaults (dmPolicy="pairing", groupPolicy="allowlist")
- `IrcAccountSchema` - adds validation requiring allowFrom=["*"] when dmPolicy="open"
- `IrcConfigSchema` - top-level config allowing multiple accounts
- `IrcChannelConfigSchema` - final schema with UI hints

### /extensions/irc/src/config-ui-hints.ts
User-facing hints for configuration:
- IRC section explanation
- dmPolicy explanation with security guidance
- NickServ configuration field hints
- Enabled/disabled status hints
- Password and passwordFile guidance

---

## PART 4: ACCOUNT RESOLUTION

### /extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts
Account resolution and configuration merging:
- `resolveIrcAccount()` - resolves merged config from root + accounts section with environment variable fallback
- `listEnabledIrcAccounts()` - filters enabled accounts
- Supports environment variables: IRC_HOST, IRC_PORT, IRC_TLS, IRC_NICK, IRC_USERNAME, IRC_REALNAME, IRC_PASSWORD, IRC_CHANNELS, IRC_NICKSERV_PASSWORD, IRC_NICKSERV_REGISTER_EMAIL
- Password resolution priority: env > passwordFile > config
- NickServ password resolution with file and env support
- Returns `ResolvedIrcAccount` with merged config and password source tracking

---

## PART 5: PROTOCOL AND NETWORK LAYER

### /extensions/irc/src/protocol.ts
IRC protocol parsing and sanitization (470 lines):
- `parseIrcLine()` - parses raw IRC protocol lines into structured format
- `parseIrcPrefix()` - extracts nick/user/host from IRC sender prefix
- `sanitizeIrcOutboundText()` - removes control characters and escapes
- `sanitizeIrcTarget()` - validates channel/nick targets
- `splitIrcText()` - chunks text to max character limit (smart word-breaking)
- `makeIrcMessageId()` - generates UUID for message tracking
- Literal escape decoding for `\r`, `\n`, `\t`, `\0`, `\xHH`, `\uHHHH`

### /extensions/irc/src/control-chars.ts
Control character detection and removal:
- `isIrcControlChar()` - detects ASCII control chars (0x00-0x1F, 0x7F)
- `hasIrcControlChars()` - checks if string contains any control chars
- `stripIrcControlChars()` - removes all control characters

### /extensions/irc/src/client.ts
Low-level IRC client implementation (440 lines):
- `connectIrcClient()` - async connection with timeout and error handling
- Handles nick collision recovery via NickServ GHOST
- Fallback nick generation (e.g., `openclaw_` suffix)
- PRIVMSG chunking at configurable character limit
- NickServ IDENTIFY and REGISTER command building
- Auto-JOIN channels on connect
- Responds to PING with PONG
- Event callbacks: onPrivmsg, onNotice, onError, onLine
- Returns `IrcClient` with methods: nick getter, isReady(), sendRaw(), join(), sendPrivmsg(), quit(), close()

### /extensions/irc/src/connect-options.ts
Connection options builder:
- `buildIrcConnectOptions()` - converts `ResolvedIrcAccount` to `IrcClientOptions`
- Merges account config with runtime overrides

---

## PART 6: NORMALIZATION AND ALLOWLISTING

### /extensions/irc/src/normalize.ts
IRC identifier normalization and allowlist matching (124 lines):
- `isChannelTarget()` - checks if target starts with `#` or `&`
- `normalizeIrcMessagingTarget()` - parses `irc:`, `channel:`, `user:` prefixes
- `looksLikeIrcTargetId()` - validates IRC identifier format
- `normalizeIrcAllowEntry()` - lowercase normalization with prefix stripping
- `normalizeIrcAllowlist()` - batch normalize array of entries
- `formatIrcSenderId()` - formats message sender as `nick!user@host`
- `buildIrcAllowlistCandidates()` - generates matching candidates for sender
- `resolveIrcAllowlistMatch()` - wildcard and pattern matching against allowlist

---

## PART 7: POLICY RESOLUTION

### /extensions/irc/src/policy.ts
IRC policy and access gate resolution (167 lines):
- `resolveIrcGroupMatch()` - case-insensitive channel config lookup
- `resolveIrcGroupAccessGate()` - validates group policy (disabled/allowlist/open)
- `resolveIrcRequireMention()` - channel-specific or wildcard mention requirement
- `resolveIrcMentionGate()` - determines if message should be skipped (missing mention, etc.)
- `resolveIrcGroupSenderAllowed()` - checks if sender matches group allowlist with fallback to outer allowlist

---

## PART 8: MESSAGE FLOW

### /extensions/irc/src/inbound.ts
Inbound message processing (364 lines):
- `handleIrcInbound()` - main inbound processing pipeline:
  1. Parse raw message body
  2. Resolve DM/group policy with pairing challenge
  3. Check sender allowlist (with dangerous name-only matching option)
  4. Check control command authorization
  5. Parse @mention patterns and explicit nick mention
  6. Check mention requirements for channels
  7. Route to agent via `core.channel.routing`
  8. Format envelope with channel/from/to/timestamp
  9. Dispatch to agent and deliver reply
- Pairing controller integration for first-time DM senders
- Support for mention pattern regex and explicit bot nick mentions
- Reply formatting with attachments

### /extensions/irc/src/send.ts
Outbound message sending (102 lines):
- `sendMessageIrc()` - sends text/markdown to IRC target
- Resolves target from argument or options
- Converts markdown tables per account config
- Adds reply metadata if replyTo specified
- Reuses existing client or creates transient connection
- Records outbound activity

### /extensions/irc/src/monitor.ts
IRC provider monitoring loop (146 lines):
- `monitorIrcProvider()` - connects to IRC and listens for messages
- Receives PRIVMSG events and filters out bot's own messages
- Converts raw IRC messages to `IrcInboundMessage` structure
- Distinguishes channels (group) vs direct messages (direct)
- Records activity (inbound/outbound)
- Calls `handleIrcInbound()` or custom onMessage callback
- Returns stop function to quit and clean up

### /extensions/irc/src/probe.ts
Connection testing (54 lines):
- `probeIrc()` - tests IRC connectivity and measures latency
- Returns `IrcProbe` with ok/error status, host/port/tls/nick, optional latencyMs

---

## PART 9: SETUP AND CONFIGURATION

### /extensions/irc/src/setup-core.ts
Core setup adapter and config patching (140 lines):
- `ircSetupAdapter` - implements `ChannelSetupAdapter` interface
  - Account ID normalization
  - Input validation (requires host and nick)
  - Config patching via scoped account helpers
  - Port parsing utility
- Setup functions:
  - `setIrcDmPolicy()` - sets top-level dmPolicy
  - `setIrcAllowFrom()` - sets top-level allowFrom
  - `setIrcNickServ()` - sets NickServ config
  - `setIrcGroupAccess()` - sets groupPolicy and group entries
  - `updateIrcAccountConfig()` - patches account-scoped config

### /extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts
Interactive setup wizard (437 lines):
- `ircSetupWizard` - implements `ChannelSetupWizard` interface with:
  1. **Intro note** - explains IRC setup, TLS recommendation, env vars, docs link
  2. **Prepare phase** - detects IRC_HOST/IRC_NICK env vars and prompts to use
  3. **Text inputs** - prompts for: host, port, nick, username, realname, channels
  4. **Group access** - channel allowlist configuration
  5. **allowFrom** - DM allowlist configuration
  6. **Finalize phase** - prompts for mention requirements, NickServ config
  7. **Completion note** - next steps and status command
- DM policy configuration helper with allowlist prompting
- NickServ interactive configuration with password and registration options
- Env var integration: IRC_HOST, IRC_PORT, IRC_TLS, IRC_NICK, IRC_USERNAME, IRC_REALNAME, IRC_PASSWORD, IRC_CHANNELS, IRC_NICKSERV_PASSWORD, IRC_NICKSERV_REGISTER_EMAIL

---

## PART 10: CHANNEL PLUGIN DEFINITION

### /extensions/irc/src/channel.ts
Complete IRC channel plugin implementation (358 lines):
- Defines `ircPlugin` as `ChannelPlugin<ResolvedIrcAccount, IrcProbe>`
- **Base configuration:**
  - ID, metadata (chat types: direct + group, media support, block streaming)
  - Setup adapter, wizard, config schema, reload prefixes
  - Account config, group mention/tool policy resolution
  - Messaging target normalization and hints
  - Target resolver (validates user vs group targets)
  - Directory adapter (lists peers from allowlist and groups from config)
  - Account status with probing support
- **Gateway:**
  - `startAccount()` - starts monitoring with passive listener
  - Records inbound/outbound activity
  - Updates account status
- **Pairing:**
  - Inline text approval via DM
  - Normalizes IRC user IDs from allowlist entries (nick-only extraction)
- **Security:**
  - DM policy resolver with allowlist formatting
  - Warning collectors (TLS disabled, NickServ registration, group policy open)
- **Outbound:**
  - Text chunking at 350 chars with markdown mode
  - Media delivery as inline attachment text
  - Reply tracking via [reply:msgId] suffix

---

## PART 11: PLUGIN SDK CONTRACTS

### /src/plugin-sdk/core.ts (first 100 lines)
Plugin SDK entry point and type exports:
- Imports and re-exports from plugin-entry.ts (comprehensive plugin types)
- Imports channel plugin types and adapters
- Defines `createInlineTextPairingAdapter()` helper for text-based pairing
- Type exports for: plugin APIs, config schemas, adapters, channel types
- Config type: OpenClawConfig

### /src/plugin-sdk/channel-contract.ts
Pure channel contract types (exported, not implemented):
- `BaseProbeResult` - status and error tracking
- `ChannelAccountSnapshot` - account status snapshot

Key channels alongside IRC:

- **telegram** - Telegram bot and channel integration
- **slack** - Slack workspace/channel integration
- **matrix** - Matrix room messaging
- **msteams** - Microsoft Teams integration

- **whatsapp** - WhatsApp chat integration
- **nostr** - Nostr protocol (DM and group notes)

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me create a comprehensive summary document:

## IRC Channel Plugin - Complete Structure Analysis

I've completed a thorough exploration of the IRC channel plugin and related plugin SDK contracts. Below is the complete file structure with full contents organized by category.

---

## PART 1: ROOT-LEVEL IRC PLUGIN FILES

### /extensions/irc/package.json
```json
{
  "name": "@openclaw/irc",
  "version": "2026.4.1-beta.1",
  "description": "OpenClaw IRC channel plugin",
  "type": "module",
  "openclaw": {
    "extensions": [
      "./index.ts"
    ],
    "install": {
      "minHostVersion": ">=2026.4.1"
    },
    "setupEntry": "./setup-entry.ts",
    "channel": {
      "id": "irc",
      "label": "IRC",
      "selectionLabel": "IRC (Server + Nick)",
      "detailLabel": "IRC",
      "docsPath": "/channels/irc",
      "docsLabel": "irc",
      "blurb": "classic IRC networks with DM/channel routing and pairing controls.",
      "aliases": [
        "internet-relay-chat"
      ],
      "systemImage": "network"
    }
  }
}
```

### /extensions/irc/openclaw.plugin.json
```json
{
  "id": "irc",
  "channels": ["irc"],
  "configSchema": {
    "type": "object",
    "additionalProperties": false,
    "properties": {}
  }
}
```

### /extensions/irc/index.ts
```typescript
import type { ChannelPlugin } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { defineChannelPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
import { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";
export { setIrcRuntime } from "./src/runtime.js";

export default defineChannelPluginEntry({
  id: "irc",
  name: "IRC",
  description: "IRC channel plugin",
  plugin: ircPlugin as ChannelPlugin,
  setRuntime: setIrcRuntime,
});
```

### /extensions/irc/setup-entry.ts
```typescript
import { defineSetupPluginEntry } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/core";
import { ircPlugin } from "./src/channel.js";

export default defineSetupPluginEntry(ircPlugin);
```

### /extensions/irc/api.ts
```typescript
export * from "./src/accounts.js";
export * from "./src/setup-surface.js";
```

### /extensions/irc/channel-config-api.ts
```typescript
export { IrcChannelConfigSchema } from "./src/config-schema.js";
```

---

## PART 2: IRC RUNTIME AND TYPE DEFINITIONS

### /extensions/irc/src/types.ts
Complete type definitions for IRC configuration and messages:
- `IrcChannelConfig` - per-channel configuration (mention requirements, tools, skills, allowlist)
- `IrcNickServConfig` - NickServ identity/registration settings
- `IrcAccountConfig` - full account configuration (host, port, TLS, nick, username, password, NickServ, channels, groups, policies)
- `IrcConfig` - root config with optional multiple accounts
- `CoreConfig` - extended OpenClaw config with IRC section
- `IrcInboundMessage` - message structure with sender info, target, isGroup flag
- `IrcProbe` - connection probe result with latency

Key features:
- Support for multiple IRC accounts per config
- DM policy control (pairing, open, disabled)
- Group policy (allowlist, open, disabled)
- Per-channel configuration with mention gating
- NickServ authentication and registration
- Text chunking configuration
- Markdown table handling
- Media attachment limits

### /extensions/irc/src/runtime-api.ts
Public runtime API barrel:
Exports from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc`:
- Status and activity recording functions
- Channel pairing controller creation
- DM and group policy resolution helpers
- Allowlist management utilities
- Message formatting and delivery
- Text chunking and inbound processing
- Plugin runtime type definitions

### /extensions/irc/src/runtime.ts
Plugin runtime store management:
```typescript
// Provides getter/setter for IRC-specific PluginRuntime instance
// Used by setup and monitor flows to access core IRC runtime services
```

---

## PART 3: CONFIGURATION AND SCHEMA

### /extensions/irc/src/config-schema.ts
Zod-based configuration validation schema:
- `IrcGroupSchema` - per-channel config with validation
- `IrcNickServSchema` - NickServ config with requirement checking (email required for register=true)
- `IrcAccountSchemaBase` - base account configuration with defaults (dmPolicy="pairing", groupPolicy="allowlist")
- `IrcAccountSchema` - adds validation requiring allowFrom=["*"] when dmPolicy="open"
- `IrcConfigSchema` - top-level config allowing multiple accounts
- `IrcChannelConfigSchema` - final schema with UI hints

### /extensions/irc/src/config-ui-hints.ts
User-facing hints for configuration:
- IRC section explanation
- dmPolicy explanation with security guidance
- NickServ configuration field hints
- Enabled/disabled status hints
- Password and passwordFile guidance

---

## PART 4: ACCOUNT RESOLUTION

### /extensions/irc/src/accounts.ts
Account resolution and configuration merging:
- `resolveIrcAccount()` - resolves merged config from root + accounts section with environment variable fallback
- `listEnabledIrcAccounts()` - filters enabled accounts
- Supports environment variables: IRC_HOST, IRC_PORT, IRC_TLS, IRC_NICK, IRC_USERNAME, IRC_REALNAME, IRC_PASSWORD, IRC_CHANNELS, IRC_NICKSERV_PASSWORD, IRC_NICKSERV_REGISTER_EMAIL
- Password resolution priority: env > passwordFile > config
- NickServ password resolution with file and env support
- Returns `ResolvedIrcAccount` with merged config and password source tracking

---

## PART 5: PROTOCOL AND NETWORK LAYER

### /extensions/irc/src/protocol.ts
IRC protocol parsing and sanitization (470 lines):
- `parseIrcLine()` - parses raw IRC protocol lines into structured format
- `parseIrcPrefix()` - extracts nick/user/host from IRC sender prefix
- `sanitizeIrcOutboundText()` - removes control characters and escapes
- `sanitizeIrcTarget()` - validates channel/nick targets
- `splitIrcText()` - chunks text to max character limit (smart word-breaking)
- `makeIrcMessageId()` - generates UUID for message tracking
- Literal escape decoding for `\r`, `\n`, `\t`, `\0`, `\xHH`, `\uHHHH`

### /extensions/irc/src/control-chars.ts
Control character detection and removal:
- `isIrcControlChar()` - detects ASCII control chars (0x00-0x1F, 0x7F)
- `hasIrcControlChars()` - checks if string contains any control chars
- `stripIrcControlChars()` - removes all control characters

### /extensions/irc/src/client.ts
Low-level IRC client implementation (440 lines):
- `connectIrcClient()` - async connection with timeout and error handling
- Handles nick collision recovery via NickServ GHOST
- Fallback nick generation (e.g., `openclaw_` suffix)
- PRIVMSG chunking at configurable character limit
- NickServ IDENTIFY and REGISTER command building
- Auto-JOIN channels on connect
- Responds to PING with PONG
- Event callbacks: onPrivmsg, onNotice, onError, onLine
- Returns `IrcClient` with methods: nick getter, isReady(), sendRaw(), join(), sendPrivmsg(), quit(), close()

### /extensions/irc/src/connect-options.ts
Connection options builder:
- `buildIrcConnectOptions()` - converts `ResolvedIrcAccount` to `IrcClientOptions`
- Merges account config with runtime overrides

---

## PART 6: NORMALIZATION AND ALLOWLISTING

### /extensions/irc/src/normalize.ts
IRC identifier normalization and allowlist matching (124 lines):
- `isChannelTarget()` - checks if target starts with `#` or `&`
- `normalizeIrcMessagingTarget()` - parses `irc:`, `channel:`, `user:` prefixes
- `looksLikeIrcTargetId()` - validates IRC identifier format
- `normalizeIrcAllowEntry()` - lowercase normalization with prefix stripping
- `normalizeIrcAllowlist()` - batch normalize array of entries
- `formatIrcSenderId()` - formats message sender as `nick!user@host`
- `buildIrcAllowlistCandidates()` - generates matching candidates for sender
- `resolveIrcAllowlistMatch()` - wildcard and pattern matching against allowlist

---

## PART 7: POLICY RESOLUTION

### /extensions/irc/src/policy.ts
IRC policy and access gate resolution (167 lines):
- `resolveIrcGroupMatch()` - case-insensitive channel config lookup
- `resolveIrcGroupAccessGate()` - validates group policy (disabled/allowlist/open)
- `resolveIrcRequireMention()` - channel-specific or wildcard mention requirement
- `resolveIrcMentionGate()` - determines if message should be skipped (missing mention, etc.)
- `resolveIrcGroupSenderAllowed()` - checks if sender matches group allowlist with fallback to outer allowlist

---

## PART 8: MESSAGE FLOW

### /extensions/irc/src/inbound.ts
Inbound message processing (364 lines):
- `handleIrcInbound()` - main inbound processing pipeline:
  1. Parse raw message body
  2. Resolve DM/group policy with pairing challenge
  3. Check sender allowlist (with dangerous name-only matching option)
  4. Check control command authorization
  5. Parse @mention patterns and explicit nick mention
  6. Check mention requirements for channels
  7. Route to agent via `core.channel.routing`
  8. Format envelope with channel/from/to/timestamp
  9. Dispatch to agent and deliver reply
- Pairing controller integration for first-time DM senders
- Support for mention pattern regex and explicit bot nick mentions
- Reply formatting with attachments

### /extensions/irc/src/send.ts
Outbound message sending (102 lines):
- `sendMessageIrc()` - sends text/markdown to IRC target
- Resolves target from argument or options
- Converts markdown tables per account config
- Adds reply metadata if replyTo specified
- Reuses existing client or creates transient connection
- Records outbound activity

### /extensions/irc/src/monitor.ts
IRC provider monitoring loop (146 lines):
- `monitorIrcProvider()` - connects to IRC and listens for messages
- Receives PRIVMSG events and filters out bot's own messages
- Converts raw IRC messages to `IrcInboundMessage` structure
- Distinguishes channels (group) vs direct messages (direct)
- Records activity (inbound/outbound)
- Calls `handleIrcInbound()` or custom onMessage callback
- Returns stop function to quit and clean up

### /extensions/irc/src/probe.ts
Connection testing (54 lines):
- `probeIrc()` - tests IRC connectivity and measures latency
- Returns `IrcProbe` with ok/error status, host/port/tls/nick, optional latencyMs

---

## PART 9: SETUP AND CONFIGURATION

### /extensions/irc/src/setup-core.ts
Core setup adapter and config patching (140 lines):
- `ircSetupAdapter` - implements `ChannelSetupAdapter` interface
  - Account ID normalization
  - Input validation (requires host and nick)
  - Config patching via scoped account helpers
  - Port parsing utility
- Setup functions:
  - `setIrcDmPolicy()` - sets top-level dmPolicy
  - `setIrcAllowFrom()` - sets top-level allowFrom
  - `setIrcNickServ()` - sets NickServ config
  - `setIrcGroupAccess()` - sets groupPolicy and group entries
  - `updateIrcAccountConfig()` - patches account-scoped config

### /extensions/irc/src/setup-surface.ts
Interactive setup wizard (437 lines):
- `ircSetupWizard` - implements `ChannelSetupWizard` interface with:
  1. **Intro note** - explains IRC setup, TLS recommendation, env vars, docs link
  2. **Prepare phase** - detects IRC_HOST/IRC_NICK env vars and prompts to use
  3. **Text inputs** - prompts for: host, port, nick, username, realname, channels
  4. **Group access** - channel allowlist configuration
  5. **allowFrom** - DM allowlist configuration
  6. **Finalize phase** - prompts for mention requirements, NickServ config
  7. **Completion note** - next steps and status command
- DM policy configuration helper with allowlist prompting
- NickServ interactive configuration with password and registration options
- Env var integration: IRC_HOST, IRC_PORT, IRC_TLS, IRC_NICK, IRC_USERNAME, IRC_REALNAME, IRC_PASSWORD, IRC_CHANNELS, IRC_NICKSERV_PASSWORD, IRC_NICKSERV_REGISTER_EMAIL

---

## PART 10: CHANNEL PLUGIN DEFINITION

### /extensions/irc/src/channel.ts
Complete IRC channel plugin implementation (358 lines):
- Defines `ircPlugin` as `ChannelPlugin<ResolvedIrcAccount, IrcProbe>`
- **Base configuration:**

  - Account status with probing support

- **Security:**

- **Outbound:**

  - Media delivery as inline attachment text
  - Reply tracking via [reply:msgId] suffix

---

Plugin SDK entry point and type exports:

15	const IrcGroupSchema = z
16	  .object({
17	    requireMention: z.boolean().optional(),
18	    tools: ToolPolicySchema,
19	    toolsBySender: z.record(z.string(), ToolPolicySchema).optional(),
20	    skills: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
21	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
22	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
23	    systemPrompt: z.string().optional(),
24	  })
25	  .strict();
26	
27	const IrcNickServSchema = z
28	  .object({
29	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
30	    service: z.string().optional(),
31	    password: z.string().optional(),
32	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
33	    register: z.boolean().optional(),
34	    registerEmail: z.string().optional(),
35	  })
36	  .strict()
37	  .superRefine((value, ctx) => {
38	    if (value.register && !value.registerEmail?.trim()) {
39	      ctx.addIssue({
40	        code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
41	        path: ["registerEmail"],
42	        message: "channels.irc.nickserv.register=true requires channels.irc.nickserv.registerEmail",
43	      });
44	    }
45	  });
46	
47	export const IrcAccountSchemaBase = z
48	  .object({
49	    name: z.string().optional(),
50	    enabled: z.boolean().optional(),
51	    dangerouslyAllowNameMatching: z.boolean().optional(),
52	    host: z.string().optional(),
53	    port: z.number().int().min(1).max(65535).optional(),
54	    tls: z.boolean().optional(),
55	    nick: z.string().optional(),
56	    username: z.string().optional(),
57	    realname: z.string().optional(),
58	    password: z.string().optional(),
59	    passwordFile: z.string().optional(),
60	    nickserv: IrcNickServSchema.optional(),
61	    dmPolicy: DmPolicySchema.optional().default("pairing"),
62	    allowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
63	    groupPolicy: GroupPolicySchema.optional().default("allowlist"),
64	    groupAllowFrom: z.array(z.union([z.string(), z.number()])).optional(),
65	    groups: z.record(z.string(), IrcGroupSchema.optional()).optional(),
66	    channels: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
67	    mentionPatterns: z.array(z.string()).optional(),
68	    markdown: MarkdownConfigSchema,
69	    ...ReplyRuntimeConfigSchemaShape,
70	  })
71	  .strict();
72	
73	export const IrcAccountSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.superRefine((value, ctx) => {
74	  requireOpenAllowFrom({
75	    policy: value.dmPolicy,
76	    allowFrom: value.allowFrom,
77	    ctx,
78	    path: ["allowFrom"],
79	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
80	  });
81	});
82	
83	export const IrcConfigSchema = IrcAccountSchemaBase.extend({
84	  accounts: z.record(z.string(), IrcAccountSchema.optional()).optional(),
85	  defaultAccount: z.string().optional(),

90	    ctx,
91	    path: ["allowFrom"],
92	    message: 'channels.irc.dmPolicy="open" requires channels.irc.allowFrom to include "*"',
93	  });
94	});
95	
96	export const IrcChannelConfigSchema = buildChannelConfigSchema(IrcConfigSchema, {
97	  uiHints: ircChannelConfigUiHints,
98	});

content.text:
All 23 files are in place. Here's a complete summary of what was created in `extensions/pulseboard/`:

---

## PulseBoard Channel Plugin — Created Files

### Root files (6)
| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Extension manifest — id `pulseboard`, env `@openclaw/pulseboard`, setupEntry + channel metadata |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin descriptor declaring channel id `pulseboard` |
| `index.ts` | Extension entry point — `defineChannelPluginEntry` wiring `pulseBoardPlugin` + `setPulseBoardRuntime` |
| `setup-entry.ts` | Setup wizard entry — `defineSetupPluginEntry(pulseBoardPlugin)` |
| `api.ts` | Public barrel re-exporting `accounts.ts` + `setup-surface.ts` |
| `channel-config-api.ts` | Exports `PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema` for external consumers |

### Source files (17)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/types.ts` | `PulseBoardBoardConfig`, `PulseBoardAccountConfig`, `PulseBoardConfig`, `CoreConfig`, `PulseBoardInboundMessage`, `PulseBoardProbe` |
| `src/runtime-api.ts` | Thin barrel re-exporting generic SDK helpers from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/irc` (all are channel-agnostic) |
| `src/runtime.ts` | `createPluginRuntimeStore` for `getPulseBoardRuntime` / `setPulseBoardRuntime` / `clearPulseBoardRuntime` |
| `src/config-schema.ts` | Zod schemas: `PulseBoardAccountSchemaBase`, `PulseBoardAccountSchema`, `PulseBoardConfigSchema`, `PulseBoardChannelConfigSchema` |
| `src/config-ui-hints.ts` | User-facing hint labels for `url`, `token`, `username`, `dmPolicy`, `configWrites` |
| `src/accounts.ts` | `resolvePulseBoardAccount` + `listEnabledPulseBoardAccounts`; env vars `PULSEBOARD_URL`, `PULSEBOARD_TOKEN`, `PULSEBOARD_USERNAME`, `PULSEBOARD_BOARDS` |
| `src/normalize.ts` | `isBoardTarget`, `normalizePulseBoardMessagingTarget` (strips `pulseboard:`, `board:`, `user:`, `@`), allowlist matching |
| `src/client.ts` | Stub `PulseBoardClient` with simulated async connect, `sendToBoard`, `sendDm`, `disconnect`; `makePulseBoardMessageId()` |
| `src/policy.ts` | Group access gate, mention gate, sender allowlist resolution — mirrors IRC policy exactly |
| `src/send.ts` | `sendMessagePulseBoard` — resolves target, converts markdown tables, delegates to live or transient client |
| `src/inbound.ts` | Full inbound pipeline: DM/group policy, pairing challenge, mention gate, agent dispatch |
| `src/monitor.ts` | `monitorPulseBoardProvider` — connects client, maps raw events to `PulseBoardInboundMessage`, calls `handlePulseBoardInbound` |
| `src/probe.ts` | `probePulseBoard` — connects stub client, measures latency, returns `PulseBoardProbe` |
| `src/setup-core.ts` | `pulseBoardSetupAdapter`, `updatePulseBoardAccountConfig`, `setPulseBoardDmPolicy`, `setPulseBoardGroupAccess` |
| `src/setup-surface.ts` | `pulseBoardSetupWizard` — full interactive wizard: env-var detection, URL/token/username/boards inputs, group allowlist, DM allowlist, mention confirm |
| `src/channel.ts` | `pulseBoardPlugin` — `createChatChannelPlugin` with `chatTypes: ["direct","group"]`, **`textChunkLimit: 4000`**, pairing, security warnings, directory, gateway |
| `src/channel.test.ts` | Vitest tests: chunker works without runtime init; `textChunkLimit` is `4000` |
